data.title: OpenAI Expands $8 ChatGPT Go Worldwide and Teases Advertising Plans
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (MSFT) AI firm OpenAI said on Friday that its lower-priced ChatGPT Go subscription is now available worldwide. The plan, which was first launched in August 2025, is now offered in more than 170 countries after seeing strong interest. According to OpenAI, users have been regularly using Go for everyday tasks like writing, learning, creating images, and solving problems. This traction ultimately convinced the company to roll the plan out globally. At $8 per month in the U.S., ChatGPT Go sits well below the $20 ChatGPT Plus and $200 ChatGPT Pro tiers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Despite the lower price, the plan includes access to OpenAI's latest GPT-5.2 Instant model, along with ten times more messages, uploads, and image creations than the free version. In addition, Go users get a longer memory and context window, thereby making the service more useful for extended conversations. As a result, OpenAI now describes ChatGPT Go as its fastest-growing and most affordable subscription offering. At the same time, OpenAI confirmed that it will begin testing advertising in the free and ChatGPT Go versions in the U.S. soon, while higher-paid tiers will remain ad-free.

Interestingly, the company said that ads could become more interactive by allowing users to ask questions directly within conversations rather than by clicking static links. This advertising move comes as OpenAI ramps up monetization plans, reportedly with help from executives who previously worked at Meta (META). Importantly, additional revenue streams could support OpenAI's plans for another massive funding round, as CEO Sam Altman has said the company is already generating well over $13 billion in annual revenue and could reach $100 billion by 2027.

Is MSFT Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $631.36 per share implies 36.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Leaps to 4-Month High as Trump Dials Down Stance on Iran
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is up by 6.7 bps to a four-month high of 4.227% as investors digest easing geopolitical tensions and President Trump's nominee to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I greatly respect the fact that all scheduled hangings, which were to take place yesterday (Over 800 of them), have been cancelled by the leadership of Iran," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Friday. Treasuries are viewed as a safe-haven asset, with easing geopolitical risks reducing demand for notes and bonds and pushing yields higher. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Why is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Up Today?

Trump also noted that he would like White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to remain in his current position. Following the comment, the odds of Trump nominating Hassett as Fed Chair dived to 17% from 31%, while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's odds rose to 61% from 34%.

Warsh is viewed by some economists as less likely to cut rates than Hassett. In other words, the 10-year Treasury yield rose as investors priced in a more hawkish Fed outlook under a possible Warsh nomination.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Foundry Plans Give Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Little Help
data.description:
Getting fully into being a chip foundry has given chip stock Intel (INTC) a lot of support in the past. There are signs that more customers are interested in signing on, and this is giving Intel quite a bit of room to run. The foundry gains only go so far, though, as Intel investors sent shares sliding nearly 2% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Intel's progress so far has been amazing. In fact, reports note, Intel is currently the third-best-performing stock in the S&P 500, up 31% to start 2026. Shares are near their highest level in two years, wiping out previous losses and making a tearing comeback.

What is behind all that optimism? Intel's own moves certainly helped. Massive firings, asset sales, and a financial picture that looks a lot brighter than it did two years ago are a big part of that. Moreover, Intel's push into foundry operations is drawing interest, and several potential customers are looking to sign on and get chips made at Intel. One big move that Intel had surprisingly little to do with, however, might be one of the biggest changes of all.

The Spillover Effect

Yesterday, we discovered that a lot of the market for Intel chips would likely come from "spill-off" customers at TSMC (TSM). And there are likely to be quite a few of these in the near-term, as new reports suggest that TSMC is closing in on the outer limits of its production capability. The demand for chips in the middle of this artificial intelligence boom is surging, and with it, comes a demand for someone—anyone—to make those chips.

Enter Intel, and its revamped foundry department. TSMC running out of room to produce means that other companies can step in. Companies like Intel, who has been preparing for this opportunity for some time now. Yes, TSMC could expand its operations, but that would take time, time that other buyers are not interested in spending. Thus, the spill-off customers are likely to become many more in number, and give Intel an opportunity to forge its own market.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 19 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 124.85% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $43.37 per share implies 8.54% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why This Stock Market Bull Is Starting to Pull Back
data.description:
Adam Parker, the founder and CEO of Trivariate Research, recently said that he has become more cautious on the stock market after several years of optimism. Speaking with CNBC, Parker explained that expectations are now so high that the downside risk has increased. In his view, companies are punished far more for missing earnings estimates than they are rewarded for beating them, which makes the current setup less forgiving for investors. As a result, Parker said he prefers to lean into healthcare stocks (XLV) as a way to reduce risk.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

He argued that healthcare offers valuable diversification in a market that is heavily dominated by technology and AI-related themes. Although this positioning initially underperformed, recent results have improved, which increased his confidence in staying overweight in the sector. At the same time, he described the S&P 500 (SPY) as being "kind of two-thirds an AI ETF," meaning that the market's direction now depends heavily on how the AI trade performs. For that trade to continue working, Parker said that capital spending must land in a "Goldilocks" zone.

More specifically, spending needs to rise enough to support strong returns, but not so much that it creates worries about oversupply or wasted investment. Notably, he pointed to Taiwan Semiconductor's recent results as a positive sign and called the company disciplined and critical to the global tech ecosystem. Still, earnings expectations remain demanding, with tech growth projected at 30% and overall market growth at 15%, with much of it pushed into the second half of the year. Therefore, Parker warned that pressure will likely build as the year progresses.

Is SPY Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SPY stock based on 411 Buys, 82 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SPY price target of $806.11 per share implies 16.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Rocket Lab Stock (RKLB) Soars as Top Morgan Stanley Analyst Upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Rocket Lab (RKLB) stock soared more than 8% on Friday, at the time of writing, as top Morgan Stanley analyst Kristine Liwag upgraded the space launch services provider to Buy from Hold and boosted the price target to $105 from $67. Morgan Stanley has an "attractive industry view" on space technology companies and expects the tailwinds that drove 2025 outperformance to continue this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Space stocks AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) and Intuitive Machines (LUNR) jumped 16% and 13%, respectively, following Morgan Stanley's favorable comments. Moreover, AST SpaceMobile secured a "prime contract position" on the U.S. Missile Defense Agency's Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) program.

Morgan Stanley Turns Bullish on RKLB Stock

Liwag highlighted that Rocket Lab is seeing growing market recognition and is being viewed as a "strategically-important, multi-faceted space company with credible long-term growth optionality."

The 5-star analyst contended that while RKLB stock's valuation has risen significantly, it is justified, given consistent launch execution and a credible entry into a capacity-constrained medium-lift market. Liwag also cited growing commercial and government demand and better visibility into long-term revenue as the reasons for the stock's premium valuation.

Additionally, Liwag considers Rocket Lab's $816 million Space Development Agency (SDA) Tranche 3 Tracking Layer award as a key win. She added that this contract further demonstrates the company's ability to compete and win against more established Defense Primes. Moreover, the analyst expects Rocket Lab's SDA win to improve its positioning for future Golden Dome opportunities.

Is Rocket Lab a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Rocket Lab stock based on seven Buys and three Holds. The average RKLB stock price target of $76.63 indicates 22.5% downside risk from current levels. RKLB stock has rallied more than 290% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Slips Despite Latest Union Agreement
data.description:
Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) has patched a lot of the holes in its labor of late. It has an agreement with the union in Washington from over a year ago. The St. Louis unions are settled from November, and now, Boeing has reached a tentative agreement with the Society of Professional Engineering Employees in America (SPEEA). This news proved less than helpful for investors, though, as shares turned down fractionally in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, this was the SPEEA chapter operating in Wichita, Kansas, representing around 1,600 workers at the fuselage supplier, Spirit AeroSystems. The negotiating team for the chapter recommended the membership accept the new contract, and actually recommended it on a unanimous basis.

Reports note that the new contract represents a substantial improvement over the old agreements, complete with "…better medical benefits, better dental benefits, more vacation time and a decent set of salary pools for raises." Boeing offered a $6,000 ratification bonus, a 20% increase to wage pools over a five-year period, a 50% increase in promotional funds, and a 10% match to 401(k) funds, though that will not start until 2027.

A New Freighter?

While plenty of attention has been focused on the arrival of the 737 MAX 10, there is one idea that is going much less noted. Some are wondering if, perhaps, Boeing will bring out at 787 Converted Freighter, otherwise known as a 787BCF.

The time is right for a new freighter, reports note. After all, the 767-300F will see its production stop in 2027 thanks to environmental regulations. Multiple cargo operators are in the market for a new class of freighter that will not break the bank in fuel costs. And the market as a whole is also hungry for a middle ground between the 777-8F and the body of narrowbody freighters already seen. This suggests a good opportunity for a 787BCF to step in. While such a plane could materialize later, there are no concrete plans to actually put one into production right now.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 44.8% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $256.54 per share implies 3.85% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Trump Weighs Tariff on Countries that Oppose U.S. Acquisition of Greenland
data.description:
President Trump is considering imposing a tariff against countries that oppose an acquisition of Greenland by the U.S.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I may put on a tariff on countries if they don't go along with Greenland because we need Greenland for national security," Trump said at a healthcare roundtable event on Friday. He didn't immediately specify a timetable or what the rate would be.

Will the U.S. Acquire Greenland?

Trump has spoken considerably about annexing Greenland since the U.S. captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro earlier this month. He previously characterized the autonomous territory as a strategic asset given its location and potential role in monitoring international rivals, such as China and Russia.

Earlier today, a group of bipartisan lawmakers met with Greenlandic and Danish leaders to discuss U.S.-Greenland relations. Delaware Senator Chris Coons noted that the meeting resulted in a "constructive and hopeful dialogue" and emphasized that it is up to the people of Greenland to decide on their future.

data.title: CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Jumps on Update About Data Center Delay
data.description:
AI cloud infrastructure company CoreWeave's (CRWV) shares climbed about 7% on Friday after JPMorgan (JPM) said the delayed data center project that hit the stock in November is back on track.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During that month, CoreWeave's shares tanked despite topping Wall Street's estimates in its Q3 2025 earnings results. This came after CEO Mike Intrator flagged a delay at one of its third-party data center partners.

However, JPMorgan analyst Mark Murphy, providing an update on his team's recent investor meeting with CoreWeave's top executives, including Intrator, pointed out that the New Jersey-based company noted that "the site is now on schedule relative to the expectations provided during the Q3 earnings call."

Investors Question CoreWeave's Data Center Execution

Murphy's comment comes as CoreWeave faces a class action lawsuit from investors concerned about its ability to meet customer demand for data center capacity. Murphy argued that investors "underappreciate the complexity and difficulty of delivering massive-scale AI data center sites on highly expedited timetables."  

Beyond the data center delay, the analyst shared other takeaways from the meeting, noting that CoreWeave stressed that AI cloud computing demand among hyperscalers remains "unsolvable," with demand still outstripping supply. Furthermore, Murphy noted that CoreWeave expressed confidence that it could reach 850 megawatts of active power capacity by the end of 2025.

Why Is JPMorgan Still Bearish on CoreWeave?

On financing, the JPMorgan analyst said CoreWeave appears less exposed to changes in its corporate borrowing costs because about 90% of its capital spending is funded with asset-based debt secured by its data center assets.

Nonetheless, Murphy reaffirmed his Hold rating on CRWV and set a price target of $110, implying about 16% upside from the closing price of $95.01 on Thursday. The analyst argued that "there could be a wide range of outcomes" for CoreWeave, warning that its shares "would probably suffer disproportionately" if economic volatility picks up and investors rotate into safer assets.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, CoreWeave's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 12 Buys and 13 Holds issued over the past three months.

However, the average CRWV price target of $123.23 implies about 23% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Outage Hits Elon Musk's X as More than 100,000 Users Report Problems
data.description:
Elon Musk's X suffered a service outage on Friday morning, which prevented many users from accessing the social media platform. As the issue spread, people reported seeing error messages and loading screens that would not refresh. At the same time, outage-tracking sites showed a quick increase in problem reports, suggesting that the disruption was a widespread issue rather than an isolated event.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to reports cited by Variety, users were met with messages such as "Something went wrong. Try reloading," which were often followed by a spinning icon that never resolved. In addition, data from Downdetector showed that complaints surged shortly after 10:10 a.m. ET, briefly eased around 10:35 a.m., and then returned minutes later. By 10:52 a.m. ET, more than 100,000 users had reported problems, with 56% tied to the mobile app, 33% to the website, and about 10% related to server connections.

Importantly, Friday's disruption was not a one-off event. Indeed, over the past two years, X has experienced several notable outages. This includes a large incident in November that was caused by internal server errors and Cloudflare (NET)-related issues. Moreover, in March 2025, the platform faced multiple short outages that lasted about 45 minutes and affected tens of thousands of users in the U.S. and UK. Similar problems were also recorded in August 2024 and July 2023.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 10.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
